content.text:
<user_query>
In  docs/development-plan-v1.md I have done prompts T1 to T14. I was expecting a UI with a simplified workflow. It's a test UI. When do I get a UI that I can share with others?
</user_query>

Checking the development plan for milestones after T1–T14 and when a shareable UI is planned.

[REDACTED]

Here is how the plan frames it.

## What you have after T1–T14

T1–T14 are exactly **Stage 0.5**: first **production-capable** slice (list, detail, playback, capture stepper, draft/retry, tests). The capture flow is intentionally **reduced** for 0.5—for example T11 is written as including a **simplified / static prompt step**, not the full AI-guided experience.

So feeling like a “test UI” with a simplified workflow matches the **0.5** scope in the plan, not a mistake in sequencing.

## When can others use it?

**Sharing is mostly deployment and access, not the next feature ticket.**

- Others get a URL they can open when **API + web are deployed** (staging or production), DNS/HTTPS are set, secrets are configured, and people have **valid JWTs** from whatever identity setup you use with Dashboard (the Memories app does not replace that shell).
- The plan ties **go-live readiness** to **Section 12.5** (manual matrix sign-off for 0.5) and **T22** (release checklist: staging → prod, flags, BAAs where relevant, dashboards, etc.). Until those are done, what you have is mainly **local/dev** or an informal deploy—not the documented production gate.

So: **you can share a hosted build as soon as you deploy it and give testers accounts/tokens**; the **formal** “shareable production slice” in the doc is **after T22 + §12.5** for Stage 0.5.

## When does the UI get “fuller” (less simplified)?

Later prompts add product depth, not the minimum bar for “others can try it”:

| Want | Plan |
|------|------|
| Multiple photos | **T15** (Stage 0.6) |
| Upload audio file | **T16** (0.7) |
| Transcription + UI states | **T17–T18** (0.8) |
| AI `suggest_prompt` instead of static copy | **T20** (1.0) |
| Tags polish | **T21** (1.0) |

**Summary:** T1–T14 delivered the **real 0.5 product slice**, which is deliberately simpler. You get a **shareable** UI when it is **hosted** and users have **auth**; you get the **fuller workflow** by executing **T15+** (and **T20** specifically for the dynamic prompt path). The **official** production-ready share per the plan is **T22 + manual matrix** on top of that.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
This UI has the steps but it doesn't look like the design in docs/memories-user-workflow-v1.md . In what epic will it look like that?
</user_query>

Checking the workflow doc and how it maps to epics in the development plan.

[REDACTED]

Here is how the docs line up.

## There isn’t one epic that says “match the PNGs”

`docs/memories-user-workflow-v1.md` is the **hi-fi reference** (screenshots + ASCII): progress chrome, “Facilitating for …”, Guide prompt card, mic + **video / type** alternates, review **waveform + tags**, success copy, and a **list inside a client shell with tabs**.

The development plan never assigns “visual parity with those screenshots” to a single epic. **E5** (`T10`–`T12`) was scoped to the **same routes and `?step=` flow** as that doc, while **T11** explicitly allows a **static/simplified prompt** and Stage **0.5** defers several things the screenshots imply.

So the mismatch you see is expected from the plan: **same steps and URLs**, not **same layout and features as every panel**.

## What pulls the UI toward that document (by epic)

| Workflow visual / behavior | Where the plan puts it |
|----------------------------|-------------------------|
| **“Ohana Guide suggests”** dynamic prompt (**FR-015**) | **E11** — **T20** (Stage 1.0) |
| **Tags** on review (**FR-016**) | **E11** — **T21** (Stage 1.0) |
| **Video** alternate on prompt/record | **E10** — **T19** (Stage 0.9) |
| **Transcription / “type or transcribe”** style behavior | **E9** — **T17**/**T18** (Stage 0.8); file paths also relate to **E8** **T16** |
| Richer **photo** UX (e.g. multiple images / gallery) | **E7** — **T15** (Stage 0.6) |
| **Client header + Overview \| Journey \| Memories tabs** | Called out as **outside** this repo: full Ohana/Dashboard shell is **out of scope** in Section 4 of `development-plan-v1.md`. Memories here is expected to ship **Memories list + capture**, not that surrounding chrome. |

**NFR-012** (simplicity / elder UX) and **`design-wireframe-v1.md`** are cited for accessibility and UX review along **T11**/**T15**, but that’s **quality bar**, not “ship the screenshot pixel-perfect in epic X.”

## Short answer

- **Structural** alignment with `memories-user-workflow-v1.md` (**routes / steps**) is **E5** (what you already finished through **T11**/**T12**).
- **Looking like** those screenshots end-to-end is **spread across E7 → E11** (and integration with the **Dashboard** shell for the tabbed list), not one epic name in the plan.

If you want a single milestone that gets you closest to the **capture** screenshots (prompt card + tags + polish), the plan points at **E11** (**T20** + **T21**), with **video** and **transcription** layers added in **E10** and **E9** respectively.

[REDACTED]
